mission.title: Install and Configure Carvel on Kubernetes
mission.description: Ytt is a YAML templating tool that allows you to manage complex configurations using reusable templates and structured YAML. Installing ytt helps streamline your configuration management process, making it easier to handle environment-specific values and custom validations.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for ytt to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Apply ytt Manifests
steps.description: Download and apply the ytt manifests from the GitHub repository.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description: Check that the ytt pods are running successfully.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the ytt pod running in the 'carvel' namespace, and you will be able to access it locally through port 8080. You can also verify that resource limits are set correctly for the deployment.

uninstall.title: Remove ytt Resources
uninstall.description: Delete the ytt resources and the namespace if no longer needed.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ytt
upgrade.description: To upgrade ytt, apply the new version's manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: Check the pod logs for errors using: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ytt`.

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas Exceeded
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that your namespace has enough resources available. Check with: `kubectl describe quota -n ytt`.

troubleshooting.title: Configuration Errors
troubleshooting.description: Validate your YAML files using ytt's built-in validation before applying them.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
